topics.title: Psilocybin and Brain Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin and related psychedelics alter brain activity, functional connectivity, and large-scale cortical dynamics. It also includes EEG- and network-based analyses used to characterize reorganization, entropy, and spatiotemporal brain dynamics.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal Change and Midlife Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual, menopausal, and hormone-therapy related changes influence brain structure, connectivity, and function across midlife and later life. It includes studies of memory, emotion regulation, hippocampal measures, and neural responses to hormonal modulation.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation in Parkinsonian Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation for Parkinson disease, especially STN-targeted therapy, programming, and patient response. It also highlights network-based and personalized modeling approaches for optimizing stimulation outcomes.

keywords: growth charts for regional resting cerebral blood flowacross
topics.title: Lifespan Cerebral Perfusion Mapping
topics.description: This cluster focuses on measuring and modeling cerebral blood flow and perfusion across development and aging, using ASL/PET approaches and normative reference models. It highlights lifespan changes, regional organization, and asymmetry in brain perfusion, including thalamic patterns and early-life growth trajectories.

topics.title: Neuroimaging Changes After Traumatic Brain Injury
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain structural and functional alterations associated with traumatic brain injury, concussion, and repeated head impacts. It emphasizes multimodal neuroimaging and network-level changes, including cortical, default mode, and limbic system disruptions.

topics.title: PTSD Network and Biomarker Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on PTSD and related comorbid or combat-related presentations, with emphasis on how brain network organization, structural connectivity, and white matter features differ across affected groups. It also highlights biomarker-oriented findings, including neurotransmitter and metabolite associations and prediction of early PTSD outcomes.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma Surgery and Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on glioma-related brain network alterations, especially functional reorganization and connectivity changes around tumor location and after surgery. It also highlights efforts to predict postoperative deficits and functional recovery using preoperative network features and lesion-related disconnection.

topics.title: Human Claustrum Structure and Function
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the human claustrum, emphasizing its morphology, microstructure, volume, and connectivity. The abstracts also link claustrum properties to development, cognition, schizophrenia, trigeminal neuralgia, and visual responses.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound Thalamotomy for Essential Tremor
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy targeting the VIM for essential tremor, with emphasis on motor and symptom improvement. The studies also examine connectivity fingerprints, cerebellothalamic networks, and longitudinal functional and structural changes linked to durable outcomes.

keywords: neurocomputational mapping of social norm transgression
topics.title: Social Norm Transgression in Frontotemporal Dementia
topics.description: This cluster centers on how frontotemporal dementia, especially behavioral variant forms, relates to disinhibition, criminal behavior, and impaired social cognition. It also includes neuroimaging and computational findings on fronto-striatal dysfunction, neural response abnormalities, and processing of social norm violations.

topics.title: Normative Modeling for Neuroimaging Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches in human neuroimaging, including EEG, DTI, and resting-state magnetoencephalography. The work focuses on calibrating models, mapping deviations, and identifying heterogeneous or transdiagnostic brain subtypes.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
topics.title: Multisite Harmonization in Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods and tools for correcting site and batch effects in neuroimaging and related multi-site datasets. It emphasizes harmonization efficacy, standardized assessment, and multivariate or machine learning approaches for improving cross-site comparability.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
paramagnetic and diamagnetic mri markers of cognitive decline
topics.title: Quantitative MRI markers in multiple sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster focuses on quantitative MRI measures used to characterize structural, myelin, and iron-related brain changes in multiple sclerosis. It also includes normative and lesion-based approaches for prognosis, disease severity assessment, and cognitive decline prediction.

keywords: long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
topics.title: Exercise Effects on Brain Connectivity and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how exercise and related interventions alter brain connectivity, white matter properties, and cortical network organization across age groups. It also emphasizes links between these neural changes and cognitive gain, including effects in healthy older adults and sedentary young adults.

keywords: brain age gap reduction following exercise mirrors clinical improvements
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Aging Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain age estimation and prediction models, including neural network approaches and brain-age gap measures. It also links these measures to aging, cognitive performance, and clinical interpretation across adult and aging populations.

keywords: mri base spinal cord morphology link
personalized spinal cord dynamic functional connectivity
potential of personalized spinal cord dynamic functional connectivity across regions and pathology
convolutional neural network base spinal cord volume quantification
topics.title: Spinal cord and cortical reorganization in disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on how spinal cord pathology and treatment relate to structural and functional reorganization across the cervical cord and cortex. It also includes quantitative MRI and neural-network approaches for measuring spinal cord morphology and connectivity in conditions such as degenerative cervical myelopathy and fibromyalgia.

keywords: local correlation of brain function in youth onset
topics.title: Brain Changes in Diabetes and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster focuses on structural and functional brain alterations associated with type 2 diabetes and prediabetes, including youth-onset disease and glycemic state. It also highlights links to cognitive impairment, cognition, and region-specific brain changes measured with MRI.

topics.title: Structural Biomarkers in REM Sleep Behavior Disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and microstructural brain abnormalities in REM sleep behavior disorder, especially markers linked to phenoconversion and Parkinson disease risk. Studies highlight brainstem, gray matter, white matter, cholinergic, and glymphatic correlates of disease progression.

topics.title: Neurovascular and Network Effects of Long COVID
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurological and vascular alterations associated with long covid across adults and pediatric cases. It highlights cognitive fog, altered brain metabolism and perfusion, network dynamics, and EEG-based biomarkers.

keywords: the effect of iron deposition on cognitive fatigue
topics.title: Neuroimaging of Fatigue in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue in multiple sclerosis, emphasizing how multimodal MRI and related neuroimaging markers can explain symptom severity and patient status. It also highlights network localization, functional connectivity, and iron deposition as candidate mechanisms underlying fatigue-related changes.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how ketamine administration alters neural dynamics, functional connectivity, and perception across healthy adults and major depression. It also includes dose-dependent pharmacodynamic effects and biomarkers of therapeutic response.

keywords: genetic correlation of cortical folding patterns
topics.title: Cortical Folding Across Development and Aging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how cortical folding patterns and related morphometric features change across development and aging in the adult human brain. It also highlights genetic correlation, normative modeling, and links to functional brain networks.

topics.title: Hallucinations in Parkinsonian and Lewy Body Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on visual hallucinations and related minor hallucinations in Parkinson disease and Lewy body disease, with emphasis on disease progression and patient variability. The abstracts highlight altered connectivity, structural changes, and metabolic/brainstem abnormalities as potential neural mechanisms underlying hallucinations.

keywords: the impact of bioenergetic challenges on functional brain dynamics
topics.title: Bioenergetic Challenges and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glucose-related manipulations and other bioenergetic challenges alter functional and metabolic brain responses. The abstracts emphasize neurometabolic mapping, fMRI/BOLD measures, and network-level reorganization including default mode network effects.

keywords: striatal social prediction error tracking moderates susceptibility
topics.title: Adolescent Brain Signatures of Substance Use Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent brain development, functional connectivity, and neuroimaging biomarkers relate to later substance use risk and trajectories. It also highlights social and familial influences, including cannabis use, nicotine use, and substance use disorders.

topics.title: Neuroimaging Biomarkers of Alcohol Use Disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging studies of alcohol-related behaviors and their neural correlates, especially reward, inhibitory control, and negative emotion processing. It also includes predictive and biomarker-oriented analyses using functional connectivity and multimodal data.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
central neural actions of ibogaine in opioid use disorder
topics.title: Craving and Connectivity in Addiction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on craving, abstinence, and cue reactivity across substance use disorders, especially methamphetamine, opioid, alcohol, and cannabis use disorders. It highlights functional connectivity and neuroimaging approaches for understanding addiction-related circuitry and treatment effects.

keywords: csf cytokine patterns mirror striatal atrophy
topics.title: Glymphatic Dysfunction in Neurodegenerative Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on glymphatic system alterations and related diffusion MRI, CSF, and protein biomarkers across brain aging and neurodegenerative conditions. It links glymphatic dysfunction to disease burden, cerebral small vessel disease, and Alzheimer's-related pathology.

keywords: performance for altered brain mri classification
topics.title: Deep Learning for Neuroimaging Disease Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on using deep learning and convolutional neural networks to classify neurodegenerative diseases from MRI and related neuroimaging data. It also includes explainability and performance evaluation for reliable clinical diagnosis.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Consciousness and anesthesia network dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how anesthetic agents alter consciousness, anesthesia depth, and EEG-derived complexity measures. It emphasizes thalamocortical and network mechanisms, including functional connectivity, periodic and aperiodic EEG components, and brain network modeling.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Neural Signatures of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain, pain perception, and analgesia, with emphasis on evoked and experimental pain and related clinical phenotypes such as widespread pain and central sensitization. It also highlights brain-based markers and network pathways, including fear-avoidance mechanisms and morphometric differences in human pain.

topics.title: Parkinson’s Disease Network Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson’s disease and related clinical subtypes, emphasizing neuropsychiatric and motor symptoms, dementia, and anxiety. It also highlights brain network dynamics, multimodal connectivity patterns, and biomarker-oriented analyses using resting-state fMRI and MEG.

topics.title: MRI Biomarkers of Parkinsonian Nigral Degeneration
topics.description: The cluster centers on imaging biomarkers for detecting substantia nigra degeneration in Parkinson disease, especially early disease and subtype-related changes. It emphasizes neuromelanin MRI, quantitative MRI, iron accumulation, and microstructural alterations linked to motor symptoms.

keywords: hemodynamic response function vary across anatomical location and pathology
spatial neural modeling of hemodynamic response functions
joint learning of dynamic convolution kernels and
topics.title: Modeling Hemodynamic Response Variability in fMRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how hemodynamic response functions vary across anatomy, pathology, and experimental context, and how they can be modeled with data-driven approaches. It includes methods for improving predictive power in fast fMRI and related applications such as spinal cord, auditory, and resting-state studies.

keywords: resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
topics.title: CSF-BOLD Coupling and Glymphatic Dynamics
topics.description: These studies examine how cerebrospinal fluid oscillations and flow relate to BOLD dynamics, cardiac pulsatility, and global neural activity. The cluster focuses on coupling patterns in resting-state and sleep-related brain physiology, including implications for glymphatic function.

topics.title: Adolescent Sleep and Brain-Cognition Links
topics.description: This cluster centers on large-scale adolescent and preadolescent studies linking sleep patterns and sleep disturbances to brain structure, functional connectivity, and cognitive outcomes such as attention and language cognition. It also highlights longitudinal associations between poor sleep, social jetlag, and developmental changes in cortical structure.

topics.title: White Matter Hyperintensities and Vascular Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities, their regional patterns, burden, and trajectories in aging and vascular risk contexts. It also links these lesions to white matter integrity, perivascular spaces, and broader neurodegenerative or cognitive implications.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity and Stimulation Responses
topics.description: This cluster centers on how electrical and auditory stimulation shapes brain connectivity and evoked responses across cortical and subcortical regions. It also includes work on excitability, effective connectivity, and links to subjective states and regional brain organization.

topics.title: Locus Coeruleus Imaging and Connectivity
topics.description: These abstracts focus on imaging and characterizing the locus coeruleus in humans, using multimodal MRI and related contrast measures to assess its activity and integrity. The cluster also links locus coeruleus properties to connectivity and cognitive state-related functions such as sleep and cognition.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature
intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: Striatal Connectivity in Impulsive and Compulsive Behaviors
topics.description: This cluster centers on impulsivity and compulsivity across adolescent and clinical contexts, including gambling disorder, online gaming, cannabis use, and comorbid HIV. It emphasizes striatal and thalamic connectivity signatures and related network alterations linked to behavior.

keywords: paired generation of healthy and disease brain biomarker profiles
foundation models identify eeg biomarkers for alzheimer 's
topics.title: Foundation Models for Brain Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on foundation models and deep learning frameworks for identifying and generating brain biomarker profiles from MRI, fMRI, and EEG data. Applications include Alzheimer’s disease, frontotemporal dementia, and broader structural brain health analysis.

keywords: nonlinear eeg complexity reveal sleep relate neural signatures
topics.title: Sleep, Aging, and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on sleep-related neural signatures and brain dynamics, including EEG complexity, functional connectivity, and glymphatic and cerebrospinal fluid interactions. It also links sleep measures with aging, healthy older adults, and memory-related changes.

topics.title: Brain Dynamics and Sleep Stage Transitions
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain activity, connectivity, and neurovascular signals change across sleep and wake states, especially during NREM and REM sleep. It also includes sleep-stage-specific phenomena such as spindles, K-complexes, and dynamic co-activation patterns measured with multimodal recordings.

keywords: multimodal imaging signatures of cognitive decline
topics.title: Spatiotemporal Subtyping of Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying disease subtypes and progression patterns in neurodegenerative disorders using machine learning, deep learning, and multimodal imaging. It emphasizes spatiotemporal trajectories, subtype-stage inference, and biomarkers linked to cognitive decline and Parkinson disease.

keywords: unsupervised modeling of eeg state space dynamics reveal latent neural profiles relate
genetic architecture of dynamic brain states identify
lifespan spectral rest state eeg normative model
topics.title: EEG Dynamics and Latent Brain States
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling EEG-derived brain dynamics to identify latent neural profiles, normative patterns, and biomarkers across populations. It also links dynamic brain states and state-space features to genetic architecture and behavior.

topics.title: Stress-related brain dynamics and resilience
topics.description: This cluster centers on how acute stress alters brain activity, connectivity, and network organization, with attention to biomarkers and canonical brain systems. It also links these neural changes to stress resilience, recovery, and mental health outcomes.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Imaging Integration Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on simultaneous and multimodal neuroimaging approaches, especially EEG, MEG, fNIR/NIRS, and optically pumped magnetometer systems. The abstracts emphasize integrating signals across modalities to study brain activity and related physiological markers.

topics.title: Multimodal Biomarkers for Alzheimer Detection
topics.description: This cluster centers on multimodal biomarker and imaging approaches for early Alzheimer disease detection, staging, and prediction of cognitive decline across the Alzheimer continuum. It emphasizes MRI, diffusion imaging, plasma proteomics, and machine learning or probabilistic fusion methods in relation to amyloid-tau-neurodegeneration processes.

keywords: resting state co activation pattern alteration
normative models of functional spreading network
recurrence networks of eeg reveal altered state space organization in alzheimer ’s disease
topics.title: Dynamic Network Alterations in Alzheimer’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on altered brain dynamics and network organization in Alzheimer’s disease and preclinical stages, including default mode network connectivity, synchronization deficits, and recurrence-based EEG analyses. It also highlights heterogeneity in mild cognitive impairment and the use of normative models and network metrics as potential functional markers.

keywords: multimodal multi field strength mri dataset
topics.title: MRI Detection of Focal Cortical Dysplasia
topics.description: These studies focus on detecting and characterizing focal cortical dysplasia and related epileptogenic malformations in focal epilepsy using MRI. They emphasize multimodal and multi-field-strength imaging approaches, including deep learning and clinical robustness across datasets.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
eeg base connectome analysis and machine learning predict surgical outcome in pediatric epilepsy
topics.title: Epilepsy Network Dynamics and Surgical Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on understanding epileptic brain networks, including altered connectivity, signal propagation, and epileptogenic zone localization across focal and drug-resistant epilepsy. It also emphasizes connectome-based and machine learning approaches to predict surgical outcomes, especially in temporal lobe and pediatric epilepsy.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
pathological proteins in preclinical alzheimer ’s disease
altered mtl pathology guided functional connectivity links
mtl pathology guided functional connectivity links synaptic function across
topics.title: Preclinical Alzheimer Connectivity and Pathology
topics.description: This cluster centers on functional connectivity changes and pathology-related network alterations in preclinical Alzheimer disease. It links medial temporal lobe and entorhinal degeneration, pathological proteins, synaptic function, and biomarker-oriented phenotyping.

topics.title: Neuroimaging biomarkers of eating disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain connectivity, neurochemical, and predictive modeling approaches used to study anorexia nervosa, obesity, and Prader-Willi syndrome. It emphasizes developmental effects, hypothalamic and striatal circuitry, and candidate biomarkers in pediatric and adolescent populations.

keywords: connectome harmonics reveal alzheimer tau connectivity alignment predict cognitive decline
distributional tau neurodegeneration modeling reveal vulnerability resilience
topics.title: Tau Heterogeneity and Cognitive Resilience in Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease heterogeneity, especially how tau pathology, amyloid, and APOE4 relate to cognitive decline, vulnerability, and resilience. It also highlights network-level and multimodal analyses linking tau connectivity alignment, functional brain health, and biomarker patterns.

keywords: amyloid vulnerable cortices exhibit higher perfusion
small choroid plexus volume before blood base amyloid conversion a longitudinal uk biobank study
topics.title: Amyloid, Atrophy, and PET in Alzheimer Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease progression across the AD continuum, linking amyloid and tau biology to brain atrophy and cortical vulnerability. It also highlights longitudinal PET and imaging phenotypes associated with conversion, individualized atrophy patterns, and preclinical cognitive decline.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
topics.title: Aging and Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging and lifespan shape brain organization, connectivity, and cognitive function across development and older adulthood. The abstracts emphasize age-related changes in brain dynamics, functional connectomes, and network stability in healthy and vulnerable populations.

keywords: hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
topics.title: Sex Differences in Functional Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how sex differences relate to brain structure, functional connectivity, and network organization across development and the lifespan. It includes comparative and contextual connectomics approaches, with attention to cognition, youth, and resting-state MEG.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Tinnitus Perception
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural correlates and brain mechanisms underlying tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes, perceptual features, and suppression. It also includes structural and neurochemical findings across auditory, thalamic, and prefrontal regions.

topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder in youth, emphasizing frontostriatal and executive network alterations, resting-state connectivity, and related brain network measures. It also includes psychotherapy and CBT outcome studies focused on neural predictors of treatment response.

keywords: dimensions of psychiatry for clinical prediction
topics.title: Machine Learning for Psychiatric MRI Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on using machine learning and MRI-based representations to classify psychiatric conditions, especially schizophrenia spectrum disorder and bipolar patient groups. It also includes work on clinical prediction, patient stratification, and interpretability methods such as saliency map.

keywords: spatial trends in brain maps evidence and recommendation
topics.title: Spatial Mapping Methods for Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain mapping and spatial analysis approaches used to study organization in the human cortex. It includes mapping associations, projection and interpolation methods, and statistical procedures such as spin tests and effective sample size estimation.

topics.title: Transcranial Ultrasound Neuromodulation in Human Circuits
topics.description: These studies examine transcranial ultrasound stimulation and related neuromodulation approaches in humans, often combined with fMRI and multimodal neuroimaging. The cluster centers on circuit-level effects, rapid neuroplasticity, and applications to specific brain networks and disorders such as Tourette syndrome.

topics.title: Neuromodulation of Brain Connectivity and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain stimulation approaches and their effects on connectivity, hemodynamics, and cognitive control across clinical and experimental contexts. It also includes individualized and analytical frameworks for optimizing stimulation protocols and interpreting neural responses.

keywords: efficacy of personalized connectivity guide brain stimulation in treatment resistant depression
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation for OCD and Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on personalized and network-guided brain stimulation approaches for treatment resistant depression and OCD, including adolescent OCD. It emphasizes connectivity-based targeting, stimulation efficacy, and related circuit effects across TMS and other interventions.

keywords: brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
functional connectivity following seizure therapy for treatment
graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
topics.title: Predicting Treatment Response in Depression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on depression and major depressive disorder, especially treatment resistant depression, with emphasis on predicting response to antidepressants and neuromodulation therapies. It highlights brain network and functional connectivity markers used for individualized targeting and outcome prediction.

keywords: dorsal striatum activity reflect aging and inhibitory failure across the adult lifespan
topics.title: Aging, Control, and Memory Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging affects executive control, inhibitory processes, and memory across the adult lifespan. It also includes neural correlates such as dorsal striatum activity and resting-state or electrophysiological measures linked to cognitive change.

topics.title: Cerebellar Contributions to Memory and Adaptation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cerebellar organization and cerebro-cerebellar dynamics support episodic memory, associative learning, and adaptation. It also highlights age-related differences and the role of feedback and savings in motor and visuomotor learning.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity and State Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI and calcium-imaging approaches used to probe brain networks, connectivity, and state-dependent activity in mice and humans. It includes work on awake and resting-state imaging, large-scale network mapping, and cellular calcium signals.

keywords: bear very preterm or with congenital heart disease
topics.title: Brain Development After Early Medical Risk
topics.description: Studies focus on brain structure and developmental outcomes in neonates and preterm infants with medical complications such as hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy or congenital heart disease. Findings emphasize structural and microstructural brain alterations, hippocampal and thalamic morphology, white matter, and later cognitive outcomes.

keywords: brain structural connectome of adhd children
topics.title: ADHD Subtypes and Neurodevelopmental Differences
topics.description: This cluster focuses on heterogeneity in ADHD across children and developmental stages, linking symptom dimensions and subtypes to structural, connectivity, and electrophysiological differences. It emphasizes delayed maturation and individualized brain deviations as correlates of attentional dysfunction.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Developmental Brain Subtypes
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism spectrum disorder across childhood and youth, emphasizing developmental differences in brain organization, sensory processing, and clinical subtypes. It links autistic symptoms and traits with EEG and fMRI-based biomarkers and atypical functional hierarchy development.

topics.title: Developmental Brain Changes and Psychosis Risk
topics.description: Studies in high-risk offspring and adolescents examine how prenatal exposures, sex, and family risk relate to brain asymmetry, cortical and subcortical development, and cerebellar volume. The cluster also includes longitudinal cohort work on links between exposure histories and psychosis-related brain change.

keywords: gray white matter maturation executive function development
topics.title: Neural Development and Cognitive Control in Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain development, connectivity, and structure relate to cognitive processes in children and adolescents. It includes studies of working memory, task switching, metacognition, and training-related changes alongside measures such as gray matter volume and brain electrical activity.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
distinct dynamic processing circuits ieeg reveal neural pathway specialization
topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Dyscalculia and Numerical Network Change
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental dyscalculia and related differences in numerical processing and arithmetic fact retrieval. It emphasizes altered network integration, neural pathway specialization, and longitudinal changes associated with math gains and intervention-related reorganization.

keywords: spatiotemporal pattern of individual functional variability in youth
topics.title: Youth Functional Brain Development Variability
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how functional brain networks mature and reorganize across childhood and adolescence, with emphasis on normative developmental trajectories and individual variability. It also highlights precision mapping approaches for characterizing individualized network topography in pediatric samples.

keywords: the protective role of memory suppression
traumatic brain injury in adolescent brain cognitive development study
topics.title: Childhood Trauma, Resilience, and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster examines how childhood trauma and maltreatment relate to emotional regulation, resilience, and vulnerability across psychiatric and developmental contexts. It also highlights neurobiological and connectivity-based markers, including resting state and state functional connectivity, in transdiagnostic and normative modeling studies.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment, Adversity, and Resilience
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early life adversity, family environment, and related risk factors shape adolescent neurodevelopment and later psychopathology. It emphasizes resilience and developmental pathways linking environment, brain, and mental health outcomes.

keywords: prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
prenatal mental health early brain structure shape infant temperament
topics.title: Perinatal Mental Health and Infant Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how prenatal and perinatal adversity, especially maternal mood and depression, relates to early brain development and later child outcomes. It links longitudinal brain measures, connectivity, and structural features to infant temperament, postpartum depression, and early autism risk.

topics.title: Motor Representation and Action Observation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human motor system represents and controls actions such as grasping, finger movement, and hand actions, including contributions from primary motor cortex and body representation networks. Several abstracts also examine how observation, imitation, and sensory feedback shape these motor representations.

keywords: progressive multi stage framework for longitudinal infant brain network completion
topics.title: Infant Connectome Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional and structural brain networks emerge and transform across early life, including neonatal, infant, and early childhood stages. It emphasizes longitudinal connectome analysis, developmental dynamics, and structure-function coupling in typical and preterm populations.

topics.title: Preterm Brain Development and Network Function
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neurodevelopmental outcomes in preterm-born infants and children, especially cognition, language, and sleep-related markers. It highlights altered brain connectivity, network dynamics, and microstructural development as predictors of later functioning.

keywords: variability dependent disruption of structural functional coupling
topics.title: Reward Circuitry and Anxiety-Depression Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurobiological alterations in reward circuitry and brain connectivity associated with social anxiety, anxious depression, and related symptoms. It also includes cross-disorder comparisons and vulnerability factors such as sex differences and developmental effects.

topics.title: Naturalistic Brain Dynamics and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain activity and network organization relate to cognition during naturalistic stimuli such as movie viewing and audiovisual perception. It emphasizes neural variability, typicality, and temporal dynamics across large-scale brain networks.

topics.title: Brain Subtypes and Heterogeneity in Depression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying clinically distinct depression subtypes using clustering, multimodal stratification, and predictive modelling. It emphasizes brain heterogeneity, neuroanatomical differences, and large-scale multi-cohort analyses to improve generalizability.

topics.title: Inflammation and Brain Connectivity in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on how inflammatory processes, including peripheral and acute inflammatory challenges, relate to depression and major depressive disorder. It also emphasizes associated brain network changes, such as amygdala-frontal connectivity, DMN integration, cortical excitability, and brain structure.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
longitudinal multimodal neuroimaging fusion for predicting working memory and attention
elastic net multiple kernel learning models
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging for Cognitive Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on using multimodal neuroimaging and brain connectivity integration to predict cognitive outcomes such as memory, attention, and working memory, especially in youth. It also includes longitudinal fusion and predictive modeling approaches for brain MRI and related performance measures.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: Aperiodic and Alpha Brain Dynamics
topics.description: The cluster centers on aperiodic and alpha-band neural activity and how these signals relate to brain states, cognitive engagement, and individual differences. It also includes work on brain fingerprinting and dynamic changes in EEG-related measures across tasks and states.

keywords: explore brainstem autonomic and cardiorespiratory signal interaction
topics.title: Brain-Heart Interoceptive Interaction Studies
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how cardiac and autonomic signals interact with neural activity during interoception, including heartbeat-evoked responses and heart rate variability. It also spans multimodal approaches linking ECG, fMRI, and intracranial recordings to brain regions such as the insula and ACC, with relevance to transdiagnostic markers.

topics.title: Neuroimaging of Suicide Risk in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain network dysfunction and structural connectivity changes associated with suicide risk, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts across psychiatric disorders. It highlights multimodal MRI findings in depression and bipolar disorder, including amygdala connectivity and hippocampal subfield volumes.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity Alterations in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related disorders, especially adolescent depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on brain imaging findings. It highlights structural and functional connectivity changes, white matter abnormalities, and developmental patterns linked to symptom progression and suicide risk.

keywords: idiosyncratic fine grained movie fmri patterns
comparison of topological organization of structural covariance networks
topics.title: Primate Brain Evolution and Hyperalignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on comparative primate neuroscience, especially the evolution of auditory and temporal cortex organization across humans, macaques, and great apes. It also includes fMRI-based hyperalignment and naturalistic movie analyses to study shared information and fine-grained neural patterns.

keywords: infant laminar cortical myelination associated with behavior and environment
topics.title: White Matter Development Across Childhood
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how white matter microstructure, myelination, and laminar organization change across infancy, childhood, and adolescence. It also highlights links to behavior, environment, genetics, and neurodevelopmental differences.

topics.title: Retinotopic Organization of Human Visual Cortex
topics.description: This cluster centers on the organization of human visual cortex, especially retinotopic mapping, V1/V2 structure, and functional boundaries across striate and extrastriate areas. It also links visual cortical organization to cortical geometry, folding, and wiring constraints.

keywords: sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Structural Mapping of Glaucoma-Related Neurodegeneration
topics.description: These studies examine structural and microstructural brain and retinal changes associated with glaucoma, especially optic nerve degeneration and related visual pathway anatomy. They also include developmental and normative MRI analyses of white matter and visual cortex organization across age.

keywords: automatic region delineation of the reading circuitry
densely sample single subject task fmri data
topics.title: Functional Localization of Reading Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods and applications for precisely delineating reading and language-related brain circuits, especially in ventral occipitotemporal and related cortical regions. It emphasizes individualized functional localization, dense sampling, and reproducible mapping across task fMRI datasets.

keywords: inclusive unsedated brain mri protocol for neurodiverse children with behaviours of concern
topics.title: Reducing Motion in Pediatric Brain MRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on strategies to improve brain MRI access and data quality in neurodiverse and young children, especially those with behaviours of concern. It includes training protocols, virtual reality approaches, and motion-reduction devices for unsedated scanning.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Dysfunction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how smell and body odor are represented in the brain, including neural mechanisms underlying olfactory identification and brain response. It also includes olfactory dysfunction, congenital anosmia, and related effects in patients and females.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Risky Decision Making
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural and computational mechanisms underlying decision making under risk, including reward learning, exploration-exploitation tradeoffs, and reward prediction error signaling. It also highlights dopaminergic and midbrain contributions to risky choices and reward processing.

keywords: the dieter 's dilemma attentional focus on health
homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate
neural correlates of food choice modulate
topics.title: Neural Control of Food Choice and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cognitive control, attention, and distraction shape food choice and food-related memory. It also highlights neural correlates of taste, health, and homeostatic-hedonic processing in decisions about food.

keywords: cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
music and emotion in functional connectivity
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Music Reward and Emotion
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music engagement, reward, and emotion are represented in neural and physiological responses. It emphasizes cortico-subcortical mechanisms, functional connectivity, and individual differences related to music participation and training.

topics.title: Genetic Links to Schizophrenia and Brain Structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on the genetic architecture of schizophrenia and related psychiatric disorders, emphasizing polygenic risk, shared heritability, and genome-wide analyses. It also connects these genetic factors to structural brain variation and multimodal imaging traits.

keywords: robust bold non bold ica component classification
topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI denoising and motion handling
topics.description: This cluster centers on multi-echo fMRI preprocessing and denoising approaches, especially ICA-based methods for separating BOLD and non-BOLD components. It also emphasizes motion correction and improving reliability in resting-state analyses.

keywords: brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
multi site multi modal mrs study
topics.title: Metabolic Network Gradients in Human Brain
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how cortical and whole-brain metabolic organization supports network integration and disease-related differences. It emphasizes MR spectroscopy, connectomics, and gradient-based analyses of neurochemical and metabolic signatures.

topics.title: Multiscale Brain Mapping and Modeling Tools
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods and software for multiscale, multimodal brain mapping and reproducible neuroimaging analysis. It links human and mouse neuroscience with voxelwise modeling, brain atlases, and computational toolboxes.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Patterns in Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on shared transdiagnostic features across psychiatric and neuropsychiatric disorders, especially within the psychosis spectrum and schizophrenia. It emphasizes cross-disorder structural and functional patterns, including network-level connectivity and heterogeneity.

topics.title: Network Dysregulation in Psychosis and Schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and psychosis, especially auditory hallucinations, memory, and early disease-related symptoms. The abstracts emphasize disrupted brain network communication, hippocampal coordination, dopaminergic connectivity, and broader intrinsic brain activity alterations.

keywords: adaptive interplay of action observation and mentalizing networks during ambiguous social perception
neural representation of perceived engagement during action observation
the brain network underlying social participation a multimodal
daily experiences sculpt neural representations of other an intensive longitudinal fmri study
topics.title: Social Brain Dynamics in Naturalistic Interaction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how social brain networks support social inference, navigation, and perceived engagement during naturalistic and ambiguous social situations. It also includes longitudinal and multimodal work on how daily experiences shape neural representations of social participation and relationships.

keywords: post mortem human midbrain t2*w mri
topics.title: High-Resolution Brain Vascular Mapping
topics.description: Studies of high-resolution MRI and related reconstruction methods for visualizing and quantifying human brain vasculature, including intracortical vessels and venous anatomy. The cluster emphasizes vascular mapping across major territories and links vessel structure to cortical microarchitecture.

keywords: stable latent dimension organize brain circuit
topics.title: Brain Network Structure and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how latent MRI-derived network structure and functional connections relate to cognition, intelligence, and personality traits. It also includes work on reinforcement sensitivity theory, emotion, and neuroanatomical organization in healthy subjects.

keywords: fast accurate population receptive field prf mapping
topics.title: High-Field fMRI Methods and Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-field fMRI acquisition and analysis methods, especially techniques for improving image quality, distortion correction, and BOLD sensitivity. It also includes population receptive field mapping and intrinsic functional connectivity applications.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity Imaging and Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity with ASL/BOLD/perfusion MRI approaches, often using hypercapnia or oxygen breathing paradigms. It also includes software and post-processing workflows for mapping, visualization, and analysis of cerebrovascular physiology.

topics.title: Hippocampal Memory Representation and Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how hippocampal and broader brain systems support working memory, episodic memory consolidation, and episodic simulation. It highlights neural correlates, long-axis functional division, and memory representation across encoding, retrieval, and flexible storage.

keywords: the neural underpinnings of social threat
the functional heterogeneity of the brain during threat processing
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Threat and Fear
topics.description: This cluster centers on how anxiety and threat shape fear conditioning, extinction, and amygdala-related responses across neural circuits. It emphasizes the neural underpinnings of social and general threat processing, including prefrontal regulation and autonomic involvement.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Workflows and Infrastructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure for data organization, processing, and workflow integration. It highlights BIDS-based conversion, cloud and desktop tools, and automated pipelines supporting clinical and research neuroimaging.

topics.title: fMRI Processing and Quality Assessment
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI analysis workflows, including preprocessing, quality control, and robustness across multi site studies. It also touches on brain behavior associations and variability in task and resting state measures, including work involving child data.

topics.title: Human Cortical Cytoarchitecture Atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on atlas building and cytoarchitectonic mapping of the human cortex, with related work on cortical mantle, subcortex, and standardized MNI-space representations. It also includes community resources and probabilistic surface-based atlas approaches for comparative brain mapping.

topics.title: Auditory Prediction and Speech Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the auditory cortex supports speech perception, prediction, and semantic processing across listening conditions. It also highlights neural encoding, individual differences, and developmental or learning-related changes in auditory processing.

topics.title: Reliability of resting-state fMRI connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the reliability and reproducibility of resting-state fMRI measures, especially functional connectivity estimates across repeated scans and different acquisition conditions. It also examines how scan length, cohort size, and noise correction affect stability and measurement quality.

keywords: large multi center study diffusion mri protocols
topics.title: Multisite Diffusion MRI Reproducibility and Protocols
topics.description: This cluster centers on multisite diffusion MRI studies aimed at improving protocol optimization, reproducibility, and generalizability across scanners and cohorts. It also includes diffusion kurtosis imaging methods and variability assessment for robust microstructural measurement.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language functions are lateralized across the brain and how that organization varies with handedness, bilingualism, development, and injury. It also includes studies of aphasia, stroke, neonatal injury, and white-matter pathways underlying language reorganization.

keywords: assessment of neural correlates of language processing in multilingual speakers
topics.title: Neural Language Processing in Multilinguals
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports language processing, comprehension, and speech, especially in multilingual and bilingual speakers. It emphasizes fMRI-based investigation of language networks, Broca-related regions, and neural responses during lexical production and comprehension.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
task guide cross subject neural alignment
topics.title: Brain-Model Alignment in Natural Vision
topics.description: Studies of how deep neural networks and human fMRI data relate through encoding, decoding, and representational alignment. The cluster emphasizes brain-model comparisons, natural scenes, and predictions of neural and behavioral responses.

topics.title: White Matter Tractography and Tractometry
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for reconstructing, parcellating, and quantifying white matter pathways using tractography and tractometry. It also includes individualized and normative analyses for detecting abnormalities, tracking longitudinal change, and comparing tract features across subjects and sites.

keywords: 75μm isotropic human brain ex vivo dataset
topics.title: Multimodal Human Brain Microstructure Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on integrating diffusion MRI, microscopy, and histology to map human brain microstructure and architecture at high resolution. It emphasizes ex vivo atlases, tissue fingerprints, and multimodal validation across imaging and transcriptomic data.

keywords: deep learning accelerated reconstruction of self
deep learning denoise for single excitation wideband
topics.title: Low-Field MRI and Deep Learning Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on low-field and ultra-low-field MRI applications, often compared with high-field imaging or used in portable and pediatric brain imaging. A major emphasis is on deep learning-based reconstruction, acceleration, denoising, and self-supervised methods for improving image quality and feasibility.

keywords: dynamic developmental trajectories of emotion neural modules
emotion regulation effort predicts supplementary motor area activation
topics.title: Neural and Developmental Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation is implemented in the brain, including cognitive reappraisal, acceptance-based strategies, and amygdala regulation. It also links these processes to developmental trajectories, psychological flexibility, and transdiagnostic psychopathology in adolescents and youth.

keywords: fetal brain mri free text corpora
topics.title: Reproducible Fetal Brain MRI Pipelines
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, automated, and reproducible workflows for fetal brain MRI analysis, including preprocessing, reporting, and multi-modal processing. It also highlights related fetal brain imaging resources and applications to developmental and clinical research.

keywords: normative modeling of fetal cortical folding trajectories use spectral analysis of gyrification
topics.title: Normative Mapping of Cortical Folding Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cortical folding and gyrification develop across fetal, neonatal, and lifespan stages, often using atlases, surface MRI, and normative modeling. It also includes computational pipelines and spectral analysis methods for extracting and comparing folding-related morphology.

keywords: comparative evaluation of ivim fitting methods in acute ischemic stroke
deep learning models of acute stroke severity
topics.title: Acute Stroke Severity and Progression Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on acute ischemic stroke characterization, including diagnosis, severity assessment, and monitoring infarct or ischemic progression. It also includes imaging and modeling approaches such as diffusion MRI, perfusion MRI, IVIM fitting, and deep learning for prognosis and evaluation.

keywords: fnirs evidence for consistent affective representations across viewing and recall
topics.title: Naturalistic Emotion Processing Across Modalities
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human brain represents emotion during naturalistic viewing and recall, especially with films and visual emotional stimuli. It also includes evidence from fNIRS, fPET, and FDG about consistent affective representations and metabolic responses.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Emotion Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain represents and processes emotions, especially valence, arousal, and facial or affective cues. Studies emphasize amygdala-related activity, connectivity, and neuroimaging signatures underlying emotional experience and regulation.

keywords: robust ensemble learning for practical ischemic stroke lesion segmentation
topics.title: Robust Deep Learning for Brain Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and robust MRI-based segmentation of brain lesions and substructures using deep learning, ensembles, and multi-atlas methods. A major emphasis is improving generalization across modalities, datasets, and anatomical targets such as stroke lesions, hippocampal subfields, and cortical regions.

topics.title: Benchmarking Longitudinal Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on evaluating and comparing brain MRI segmentation pipelines for longitudinal analysis, with attention to reproducibility, robustness, and test-retest stability. It includes multi-cohort and multi-dataset benchmarking of methods such as FreeSurfer and SynthSeg.

keywords: high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
pediatric adult automated mri segmentation tools
topics.title: Automated Infant and Pediatric Brain Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated MRI segmentation methods for neonatal, infant, and pediatric brain structures, with emphasis on robustness, validation, and clinical applicability. Several phrases highlight specialized pipelines and tools for choroid plexus and infant brain segmentation, often using transfer learning or age-conditioned approaches.

keywords: spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
topics.title: Neural Basis of Visual Mental Imagery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual mental imagery is represented across the brain, including variation in vividness, imagery ability, and the role of early visual, parietal, and default mode networks. It also spans topographic and spatial coding, with links to blindness, aphantasia, and the spatiotemporal dynamics of imagined content.

keywords: the neural correlates of selective attention
topics.title: Selective Attention and Visual Spatial Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how selective attention and visual spatial attention shape conscious detection, perceptual learning, and performance in visual tasks. It also includes work on neural correlates, developmental changes, and spatial biases in attention.

topics.title: Gradients and Connectivity in Prefrontal Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional organization and connectivity shape prefrontal and cingulo-frontal systems across tasks and intrinsic networks. It also highlights gradient-based and receptor-based accounts of cortical structure-function relationships, including layer-specific and anatomically grounded patterns.

topics.title: Lesion Network Mapping in Stroke and Symptoms
topics.description: This cluster centers on lesion network mapping and related approaches for linking focal brain lesions to distributed network disruptions. The abstracts apply these methods to stroke and symptom phenotypes such as cognitive impairment, dysphagia, body representation disorder, and behavioral changes.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Stroke motor recovery networks and structural disconnection
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how stroke lesion topography, structural disconnection, and network connectivity shape motor and visuomotor recovery. It highlights predictive connectivity markers and the role of contralesional and sensorimotor pathways in post-stroke motor outcome.

keywords: cortical gradients of functional connectivity from subacute
temporal signatures of post stroke neuroplasticity in lesion centre functional networks
topics.title: Post-stroke network reconfiguration and recovery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke alters functional and structural brain networks across acute, subacute, and chronic stages. It emphasizes post stroke neuroplasticity, connectome-based characterization, and prediction of cognitive and functional recovery.

topics.title: Dynamic Functional Connectivity in fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling time-varying brain connectivity and brain state dynamics in fMRI, spanning resting-state and task-based settings. It emphasizes predictive and state-space approaches such as hidden Markov models, Gaussian mixtures, and linear dynamical models for individualized and task-phase analysis.

keywords: biophysical large scale brain circuit models
topics.title: Virtual Brain Models of Stimulation Responses
topics.description: This cluster focuses on computational and biophysical modeling of brain networks to predict and analyze responses to external or direct electrical stimulation. It emphasizes virtual brain models, network propagation, and parameter estimation in large-scale circuit models.

topics.title: Dynamic Effective Connectivity in fMRI Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and estimating dynamic effective connectivity in fMRI-based brain networks, including spectral and nonlinear approaches. It also reflects work on causal inference, generative models, and brain dynamics for understanding inter-region interactions.

topics.title: Brain State Dynamics and Attractor Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain states evolve through attractor-like dynamics, state transitions, and low-energy control principles in large-scale networks. It links resting-state and hippocampal-cortical activity to frameworks such as network control theory and minimum energy transition.

keywords: microstructural basis of structure function coupling across human resting state networks
topics.title: Structure-Function Coupling in Human Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how structural connectivity and microstructural organization shape functional communication in human brain networks and resting-state systems. It emphasizes the microstructural basis, long-range interactions, and directed information flow underlying structure-function relationships.

keywords: flexible rest state fmri network inference
topics.title: Individualized Connectome Analysis and Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on connectome-based analyses of functional and structural brain networks, especially individualized parcellation, resting-state inference, and dense network methods. It also emphasizes prediction and biomarker discovery from brain network features across subjects and phenotypes.

keywords: task derive functional connectivity captures individual differences
rest task fmri network topology metric
topics.title: Individualized Functional Connectivity and Behavior
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how individualized functional connectivity and brain organization differ across people and relate to cognition and behavior. It emphasizes task and rest fMRI approaches for capturing stable and dynamic components of the connectome.
